leads.name: Lahari Neelapareddy, CPA
leads.headline: Founder & CEO | Sales Tax and Accounting for CPG & Ecom Brands
leads.comment: This is such a common problem, so much time gets lost in admin work that doesn’t drive revenue. I see this in tax compliance too, where businesses spend hours chasing paperwork instead of streamlining their processes. Firms that optimize workflows not only save time but also improve profitability. What’s the biggest workflow shift that made the most impact?
leads.reason: Founder & CEO of a tax/accounting firm serving CPG/ecom brands, describes seeing time lost to admin and compliance paperwork in their own practice — clear small firm operator with workflow pain.

leads.name: Ben Curtis, CPA
leads.headline: Cofounder/CEO @ Good Measure | Lead genuinely
leads.comment:
Karbon and granular time tracking within has played a big part in us getting some visibility on this….AND I think we have room to grow :)

Would love to see this Nick!
leads.reason: Cofounder/CEO of Good Measure (likely small accounting/advisory firm), describes using Karbon for time tracking and acknowledges room to grow on workflow optimization — own operational context.

leads.name: Donald T Shaver, MSFA, CPA
leads.headline: Owner helping growing businesses save money and plan for the future
leads.comment: Do you sell that logic tree as a template? If you can export it, I'd be willing to pay to get my hands on something that's already 80% of the way there.
leads.reason: Owner of a growing-business-focused firm actively seeking workflow tools and willing to pay for templates — clear operational need and small firm owner signal.

leads.name: Paul Medina
leads.headline: CPA - Owner of Medina Company, PSC
leads.comment:
When I went self employed, my first rule was that I would try my darndest to not work any Sunday, even April. In 8 years, I've only worked two of them. 

I eat at my desk, but I turn on some sort of streaming service so my brain disengages completely - usually something I've watched before so I don't get too engaged with it. A show I've seen zillions of times but still enjoy. 

And definitely agree that there are diminishing returns after 55 hrs, and 65 is definitely a good limit
leads.reason: CPA and Owner of Medina Company describing their own self-employment workflow habits — small firm owner managing tax season workload manually.
leads.source_author: John Ackerman, CPA, MBA, MST’s Post

leads.name: Fred Watkin
leads.headline: Forensic Accountant & Researcher in Financial Crime | Specializing in Tax Appeals, Fraud Detection & Asset Tracing | Quadrant Forensics
leads.comment:
1. I do not work Sundays. Period.
2. Saturday is by appointment only and is half day only.
3. Clients who do not bring tax stuff in before April 20 have no guarantee of being filed on Apr 30.
4. I go out for lunch every day in tax season to get a break from work.
5. Sign efile authorization. Pay the invoice. Then I file.
6. I do not take appointments Monday AM or Friday PM so that I can clear up the week or plan for it.
leads.reason: Forensic accountant describing their own firm's operational rules and workflow boundaries during tax season — clear small practice owner managing client load manually.
leads.source_author: John Ackerman, CPA, MBA, MST’s Post

leads.name: Stephanie Cunningham
leads.headline: Owner, CMO at Padgett Lakeland - Small Business Bookkeeping | Do more of what you love and less of what you don't! | Taxes-Accounting-Payroll-Advisory |
leads.comment: YES couldn't agree more. We use Cognito Forms , but same idea. Helpful for everyone.
leads.reason: Owner and CMO at Padgett Lakeland small business bookkeeping — describes using intake forms, confirming hands-on workflow management at a small firm.

leads.name: Rob Tamburri, CPA/PFS
leads.headline: Managing Partner at Balog + Tamburri, Certified Public Accountants
leads.comment: If we need to extend your 3/15 deadline returns your 1040 is automatically extended.   Period
leads.reason: Managing Partner at a named CPA firm describing their own deadline extension policy — title confirms small firm operator role with hands-on workflow management.
leads.source_author: John Ackerman, CPA, MBA, MST’s Post

leads.name: Logan Graf, CPA
leads.headline: CPA firm owner, accounting community runner, content creator, punchable face
leads.comment: I see you went down the logic tree rabbit hole lol. I love it but the UI made it soooo time consuming.
leads.reason: CPA firm owner describing hands-on experience building logic trees with time-consuming UI — operational frustration with manual/tedious workflow tooling.
